title: Katubala
intro:

Here is a paragraph based on my understanding of Katuk:

Katuk (Sauropus androgynus) is a tropical plant native to Southeast Asia, commonly known as katuk, ba, or wild spinach. The leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, and its health benefits include lowering cholesterol levels, regulating blood sugar, and reducing inflammation. The bioactive constituents of katuk leaves include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Katuk leaves are commonly prepared as teas, infusions, or decoctions, and are also used as a nutritious and healthy vegetable in various traditional dishes.
